sub_problems: How does an agent integrate an extended-duration contextual signal (weeks of customer-style exposure, months of preference accumulation) into a stable commitment when neither gradient fine-tuning nor in-context learning is the right mechanism?

What is the unit of probabilistic per-component commitment — a prompt-template slot, a routing-table entry, a per-skill threshold — and how does population-level threshold-crossing produce a sharp behavioral mode flip?

How does the runtime detect a legitimate reset event (new tenant, new policy, new role) that should dissolve a prior commitment, and how does dissolution propagate cleanly across all components that had committed?
How is commitment depth (how much exposure was integrated) measured, and how does that depth predict the resilience of the committed mode against contradicting evidence?
